calendar.matters:
Order To Show Cause Regarding Investigative Subpoenas And Interrogatories
Off Calendar Jan-04-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ADELINA CUNA, Esq. of the California Attorney General's Office (Tel: 415-510-3752) for petitioner;
Atty. LINA MILOJEVIC, Esq. Milojevic of Atkinson, Andelson, Loya, Ruud & Romo (Tel: 949-536-2226) for respondent California Farms, LLC and Dakota Layers, LLC.

Order To Show Cause Regarding Investigative Subpoenas And Interrogatories.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. The People's petition to enforce the investigative subpoena and the investigative interrogatories served on Dakota Layers, LLC is granted, and Dakota Layers is ordered to produce to the People all documents requested by the People's subpoena and answer the People's interrogatories without objection. Upon a showing that the subpoena was "regularly issued" and the interrogatories were "regularly promulgated," entry of such an order is mandatory. (Gov. Code sec. 11188 ["If it appears to the court that the subpoena was regularly issued, or the interrogatories were regularly promulgated, by the head of the department, the court shall enter an order that the person appear before the officer named in the subpoena at the time and place fixed in the order and testify or produce and permit the inspection and copying of the required papers or other items described in subdivision (e) of Section 11181 as required or answer the interrogatories without objection."].) For the reasons explained in the report and recommendation, Dakota Layers' constitutional and other arguments are misplaced. "Because the statute authorizes the [People] to issue a subpoena 'in any inquiry [or] investigation' (Gov. Code, Sec. 11181, subd. (e)), the [People] may do so for purely investigative purposes; it is not necessary that a formal accusation be on file or a formal adjudicative hearing be pending. [Citation.] Indeed, such investigations often do not result in formal charges or hearings." (Arnett v. Dal Cielo (1996) 14 Cal.4th 4, 8.) If the People ultimately bring criminal charges or enforcement proceedings against Dakota Layers, it may raise those arguments at that time.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Order To Show Cause Regarding Investigative Subpoenas And Interrogatories

Appearances via ZOOM Video-conferencing:

Attorney, Lina Milojevic of the law offices of Atkinson, Andelson, Loya, Ruud & Romo (Tel: 949.536.2226; Email: Lana.Milojevic@aalrr.com) appears as counsel for respondent/opposing party

Defendant declines stipulation to the Judge Pro Tem.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides on record as reflected in the notes of the Certified Shorthand Reporter.

Order To Show Cause Regarding Investigative Subpoenas And Interrogatories is CONTINUED to January 4, 2021 at 9:30 AM in Law and Motion Department 302.

Judge Pro Tem: Bruce Highman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Teresa Kenworthy, CSR No. 6673, tel: 530.308.2691; email: tkenworthy@gmail.com
